predecessor: omega-bridge-6 (nexus d31da1e3 — fingerprint-check subcommand added to bridge_helper.sh)
intent: Wire bridge_helper.sh fingerprint-check subcommand into bridge_health.sh full sweep as opt-in, so a single canonical sweep can also detect upstream JSON schema drift.

design.default: off (backward-compat — pre-existing callers see zero behaviour change)
design.url_lookup_strategy: hardcoded BRIDGE_FP_URLS table inside bridge_health.sh (option 1 from cycle prompt — simplest; O(1) maintenance per bridge addition; avoided per-bridge --canonical-url subcommand to limit blast radius)
design.invocation_point: after each bridge PASSes selftest (skipped on FAIL/TAMPERED/OFFLINE-only)
design.ttl_override_env: BRIDGE_FP_TTL (default 3600 — matches bridge_helper default; ops can set 86400 for cached-only validation runs to fully avoid live API hammering)

json_vs_non_json_split.rationale: 10 bridges return text/HTML/CSV/ASCII/Atom-XML payloads — fingerprint-check correctly tallies these as non_json (NON_JSON_BODY exit 2) and proceeds. Per omega-bridge-6 design, fingerprint detection is a JSON-only signal.

bridge_health_change.constraint_compliance: edit-only-bridge_health.sh (no bridge files touched, bridge_helper.sh unchanged)

new_flag.behaviour: After each bridge PASS (selftest sentinel matched), invokes `bash tool/bridge_helper.sh fingerprint-check <bridge> <url> <ttl> 1`. Tallies result by category. Emits per-bridge fp status line ('└─ fp:') in human-readable mode. Adds final __BRIDGE_FINGERPRINT__ sentinel + per-drift __BRIDGE_FINGERPRINT_DRIFT__ lines.
new_flag.compat: Zero observable change when flag absent (no extra subprocesses, no extra output, no new files written).

cached_first_run.result: 16/16 PASS; fp: checked=16 matched=0 drift=0 non_json=9 baseline_recorded=6 fetch_fail=1
cached_first_run.fetch_fail_one_off: gaia (no cached body for the canonical URL on first run; subsequent runs succeed once cache populates or non_json once body lands)

cached_second_run.result: 16/16 PASS; fp: checked=16 matched=6 drift=0 non_json=10 baseline_recorded=0 fetch_fail=0
cached_second_run.interpretation: All 6 JSON baselines stable on consecutive sweeps, no drift, no live fetches (TTL=86400 keeps cache hits).

synthetic_drift_run.method: jq-injected synthetic top-level key 'omegaBridge7DriftProbe' into pubchem cached body (water/MolecularFormula); preserved a .bak; re-ran sweep
synthetic_drift_run.result: 16/16 PASS; fp: checked=16 matched=5 drift=1 non_json=10 baseline_recorded=0 fetch_fail=0
synthetic_drift_run.candidate_row_recorded: pubchem\tcandidate:bb6ece1815485612\t<ts> in state/bridge_schema_fingerprint.tsv (per omega-bridge-6 contract — does NOT overwrite baseline)
synthetic_drift_run.post_test_cleanup: restored cached body from .bak; purged candidate row; baseline TSV returned to 6 clean rows

session_constraint_compliance.hexa_only_session: honoured for code edits — but bridge_health.sh is bash by design (Ω-cycle-canonical sweep runner). No hexa file touched in this cycle.
session_constraint_compliance.no_live_api_hammering: validation runs used BRIDGE_FP_TTL=86400 to keep cache hits; the fp-check itself only fires post-PASS; the underlying selftest sweep already runs as part of normal bridge_health behaviour and was not amplified

side_effects_to_clear: /Users/ghost/core/nexus/state/bridge_schema_fingerprint.tsv (created by first --fingerprint-check sweep; safe to delete — will be re-recorded on next sweep)
/Users/ghost/core/nexus/state/bridge_cache/* (untouched by this cycle; fingerprint-check uses existing cache via bridge_helper)
rollback.blast_radius: ZERO when flag not passed (default off); six new TSV rows + one new tally line when flag passed
rollback.reversibility: fully reversible — single bash file edit, additive flag, no schema/protocol changes

known_limitations.non_json_bridges_uncovered: 10/16 bridges return text/HTML/CSV — fingerprint-check tallies them as non_json but provides no drift signal for those. Out-of-scope for this cycle (would need text-shape monitoring, see omega_bridge_6_schema_drift.json future-work section).
known_limitations.live_fetch_on_cache_miss: If cache is cold AND BRIDGE_FP_TTL is left at default (3600s) AND cache mtime > TTL, fingerprint-check will go live via bridge_helper. Mitigated by ops setting BRIDGE_FP_TTL=86400 for offline-only runs, or by relying on the natural sweep cadence (which keeps caches warm).
known_limitations.url_table_drift: Hardcoded URL table can diverge from per-bridge selftest URL choices over time. Acceptable per cycle prompt (option 1 chosen for simplicity); future cycle could expose --canonical-url subcommand on each bridge to remove the duplication.
